assertionResults.reason: Focused Hardhat coverage passed for LendingCoreV2.depositCollateralFor(address,uint256), and the tested assertions show the beneficiary's collateral increases while the caller's collateral stays unchanged.
evidence: Command: TMPDIR=/var/tmp npx hardhat test --no-compile test/LendingCoreV2.ts --grep "credits beneficiary position, not caller" -> 1 passing: LendingCoreV2 > depositCollateralFor > credits beneficiary position, not caller

Source inspection: test/LendingCoreV2.ts:181-204 calls coreV2.depositCollateralFor(borrower.address, depositAmount) and then asserts beneficiaryAfter == beneficiaryBefore + depositAmount while callerAfter == callerBefore.
Source inspection: contracts/LendingCoreV2.sol:370-379 implements depositCollateralFor(beneficiary, amount) and credits positions[beneficiary].collateralAmount, not msg.sender.

assertionResults.reason: Focused Hardhat coverage passed for LendingRouterV2.depositCollateralFromPAS(), and the proof path shows the router deposits on behalf of msg.sender so the user position is credited while the router position remains zero.
evidence: Command: TMPDIR=/var/tmp npx hardhat test --no-compile test/LendingRouterV2.ts --grep "credits caller position, not router" -> 1 passing: LendingRouterV2 > depositCollateralFromPAS credits caller position, not router

Source inspection: test/LendingRouterV2.ts:143-168 asserts userPositionAfter.collateralAmount == depositAmount and routerPositionAfter.collateralAmount == 0 after router.connect(user).depositCollateralFromPAS({ value: depositAmount }).
Source inspection: contracts/LendingRouterV2.sol:54-65 wraps PAS and then calls lendingCore.depositCollateralFor(msg.sender, amount), explicitly crediting the original caller instead of the router.

assertionResults.reason: The focused idempotent deployment test suite passed, proving skip/redeploy classification, resume-from-checkpoint behavior, and stable manifest outputs across reruns. Limitation: this run validated the deployment core logic and deploy script wiring locally; it did not interrupt a live end-to-end deploy script process mid-run.
evidence: Command: TMPDIR=/var/tmp npx hardhat test --no-compile test/IdempotentDeploy.ts -> 12 passing, including 'resume scenario: second run skips steps deployed in first run' and 'manifest output is consistent across multiple complete runs'.

Observed terminal output from the suite shows [SKIP] ContractA / ContractB with only ContractC newly deployed in the resume scenario, then [SKIP] AccessManager / WPAS / USDC on the second complete run.
Source inspection: test/IdempotentDeploy.ts:202-233 proves resume semantics by skipping steps A and B when firstRunAddresses already contain live-code entries and deploying only step C.
Source inspection: test/IdempotentDeploy.ts:261-289 proves rerun consistency by asserting secondResult addresses equal firstResult addresses and deploy() is not called on the second run.

Source inspection: scripts/deploy-idempotent.ts:115-124 merges manifest contracts with deploy-idempotent-checkpoint.json, lines 317-324 persist the checkpoint immediately in onDeployed, and lines 472-474 clear the checkpoint after successful completion.
